recipes.title: Tagine
recipes.description: Moroccan slow-cooked stew of lamb, preserved lemon and olives in a conical clay pot, perfumed with saffron and ginger.
steps: Cube the lamb and toss with ginger, turmeric, saffron, cumin, salt and chopped herbs.
Layer sliced onions in the base of a tagine and pour over olive oil.
Place the lamb on top and add water to come halfway up the meat.
Cover and cook over very low heat (or 160°C) for 1.5 hours.
Add olives and chopped preserved lemon peel and continue 30 minutes.
Reduce the sauce to a glossy coating and serve with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Couscous
recipes.description: Steamed semolina couscous topped with seven vegetables and tender lamb in a saffron-scented broth, served on Fridays.
steps: Brown the lamb with chopped onion, spices, tomato paste and salt; pour over 1.5 liters of water.
Simmer for 1 hour, then add the harder vegetables (carrots, turnips) and cook 20 minutes.
Add courgettes, pumpkin, cabbage and chickpeas and cook 15 minutes more.
Steam couscous in a couscoussier above the broth for 20 minutes; rake with oil and water and steam again.
Pile couscous on a wide platter, hollow the center and place lamb and vegetables in.
Ladle hot broth over the top and serve more on the side.

recipes.title: Harira
recipes.description: Moroccan tomato, lentil and chickpea soup thickened with herbs and tedouira, traditionally eaten to break Ramadan fast.
steps: Brown the lamb in olive oil with chopped onion, celery and spices.
Add grated tomatoes, tomato paste and lentils and pour over 1 liter of water.
Simmer 35 minutes, then add chopped herbs and chickpeas and cook 10 minutes.
Whisk flour with 200 ml cold water to make tedouira.
Pour the slurry slowly into the simmering soup, stirring constantly until lightly thickened.
Cook 8 minutes more, season and serve with dates and lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Bastilla
recipes.description: Sweet-and-savory Moroccan pie of pigeon or chicken layered with almonds, eggs and cinnamon, wrapped in warqa and dusted with sugar.
steps: Simmer chicken with chopped onion, ginger, saffron and salt in 500 ml water for 30 minutes; shred and reduce broth.
Beat eggs into the reduced broth over low heat and stir until softly curdled.
Toast almonds, then pulse with sugar and 1 tsp cinnamon to a coarse crumb.
Layer 6 buttered sheets of warqa in a round tin, overlapping the edges.
Spread shredded chicken, then egg curds, then almond mixture; fold the edges over and top with 6 more buttered sheets.
Bake at 200°C for 25 minutes until deep gold, then dust with powdered sugar and cinnamon.

recipes.title: Mechoui
recipes.description: Whole spit-roasted lamb rubbed with cumin and saffron, slow-cooked until the meat falls from the bone, served with cumin salt.
steps: Pound garlic, salt, cumin, paprika, ginger and saffron with melted ghee to a thick paste.
Score the lamb shoulder all over and rub the spice paste in deeply.
Place in a heavy roasting tin with water in the base and cover tightly with foil.
Cook at 150°C for 4 hours until the meat is falling apart.
Uncover and roast at 220°C for 20 minutes to crisp the surface.
Pull into chunks and serve with a small bowl of mixed cumin and salt.

recipes.title: Rfissa
recipes.description: Moroccan comfort dish of shredded msemen flatbread soaked in fenugreek-spiced lentil broth with chicken and onions.
steps: Soak fenugreek seeds in warm water for 30 minutes.
Brown the chicken with sliced onions in ghee, then add ras el hanout, ginger, saffron and salt.
Add drained fenugreek and water and simmer 50 minutes.
Add lentils and cook a further 30 minutes until soft.
Tear the msemen into pieces and arrange on a wide platter.
Spoon hot broth over to soak, then top with chicken, lentils and chopped coriander.

recipes.title: Zaalouk
recipes.description: Smoky Moroccan salad of charred aubergines and tomatoes simmered with garlic, paprika and cumin until thick and jammy.
steps: Char the aubergines over an open flame for 12 minutes until blackened, then peel and chop the flesh.
Grate the tomatoes and crush the garlic.
Heat olive oil and cook the tomatoes, garlic, cumin, paprika and salt for 15 minutes until thick.
Stir in the chopped aubergine and cook another 15 minutes, smashing it into the sauce.
Finish with chopped herbs and lemon juice.
Serve at room temperature with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Msemen
recipes.description: Square Moroccan flatbreads of layered semolina dough folded with butter and pan-cooked until flaky and golden.
steps: Mix flour, semolina, yeast, salt and water into a soft dough; knead 8 minutes.
Divide into 12 balls, coat in oil and rest 30 minutes.
Press each ball into a thin transparent circle on an oiled surface.
Drizzle with melted butter and dust with semolina, then fold into a tight square.
Press the square out gently and pan-cook in a dry hot skillet for 2 minutes per side.
Stack and serve hot with honey and butter or alongside tagines.

recipes.title: Chebakia
recipes.description: Sesame-coated rose-shaped Moroccan pastries fried until crisp then soaked in honey, eaten throughout Ramadan with harira.
steps: Toast 150 g sesame and the almonds, then grind both finely with anise, cinnamon and saffron.
Mix the ground paste into the flour with egg, blossom water, vinegar and warm water; knead 10 minutes to a firm dough.
Roll out thin, cut into rectangles, slit and fold into rose shapes.
Fry in 175°C oil until deep golden and crisp, working in batches.
Warm the honey gently and submerge each fried piece for 1 minute.
Drain on a rack and roll in remaining sesame seeds while still sticky.

recipes.title: Sellou
recipes.description: Toasted flour, almond and sesame energy paste sweetened with honey, served at Ramadan, weddings and after childbirth.
steps: Toast the flour in a dry pan for 15 minutes, stirring constantly until pale gold.
Toast almonds at 180°C for 8 minutes and let cool.
Toast sesame seeds in a dry pan, then grind with anise.
Pulse the toasted almonds finely, then mix with the toasted flour, sesame, cinnamon and sugar.
Stir in melted ghee and honey to form a soft paste.
Pack into a serving dish, smooth and decorate with whole almonds; serve in small spoonfuls.

recipes.title: Tangia
recipes.description: Marrakchi clay-jar slow-cooked lamb perfumed with preserved lemon, cumin and saffron — historically buried in hammam embers.
steps: Place the lamb in a tangia jar or a heavy lidded pot.
Add whole peeled garlic, chopped preserved lemon peel, smen, oil, spices and salt.
Pour in water and seal the jar with parchment paper tied down.
Cook at 130°C for 6 hours, or buried in hot ashes traditionally.
Uncover and check the meat is meltingly tender.
Serve straight from the pot with bread to scoop the juices.

recipes.title: Khobz
recipes.description: Round Moroccan country bread with a crisp crust and dense crumb, served alongside every meal to scoop tagines.
steps: Combine flours, yeast, salt and water and mix into a soft dough.
Knead 10 minutes until smooth, then divide into 4 balls.
Flatten each into a 15 cm round and dust with semolina.
Cover and proof on a tray for 1 hour.
Slash the tops, brush with olive oil and prick with a fork.
Bake at 230°C for 18-20 minutes until the bread is dark gold and sounds hollow.

recipes.title: Briouats
recipes.description: Crispy triangular Moroccan pastries of warqa wrapped around spiced lamb or almond filling, fried golden and dipped in honey.
steps: Brown the lamb with chopped onion, coriander, cumin, cinnamon, ras el hanout and salt.
Cook until dry, then cool completely.
Cut warqa sheets into long strips about 8 cm wide.
Place a teaspoon of filling at one end and fold up into tight triangles, sealing with egg yolk.
Fry in 175°C oil until deep golden, about 3 minutes.
Drain briefly, dip into warmed honey and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Pastilla bil Hout
recipes.description: Coastal Moroccan version of bastilla filled with seasoned fish, vermicelli and herbs in crispy warqa pastry.
steps: Sauté chopped onion and garlic in olive oil, then add cumin and harissa and cook 2 minutes.
Stir in cubed fish and prawns and cook 4 minutes; season with salt and lemon juice.
Soak vermicelli in hot water for 5 minutes, drain and toss with the seafood and coriander.
Brush a round tin with melted ghee and layer 6 sheets of warqa overlapping the edges.
Spoon in the seafood, fold the edges over and top with 6 more buttered sheets, tucking under.
Bake at 200°C for 20 minutes until crisp and golden; serve with lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Bissara
recipes.description: Moroccan winter breakfast soup of pureed split fava beans, garlic and cumin, drizzled generously with olive oil.
steps: Soak the favas overnight then drain.
Cover with water and simmer with peeled garlic and salt for 60 minutes until very soft.
Blend the soup until silky, adding water to loosen.
Stir in cumin, half the paprika and lemon juice.
Ladle into bowls and drizzle generously with olive oil.
Dust with the remaining paprika and serve with bread.

recipes.title: Taktouka
recipes.description: Moroccan cooked salad of charred peppers and tomatoes with garlic, cumin and a hit of paprika, served as a side or dip.
steps: Char the peppers under a hot grill until blackened, then steam in a covered bowl 10 minutes.
Peel and seed the peppers and chop the flesh.
Grate the tomatoes and crush the garlic.
Cook the tomato pulp with garlic, cumin, paprika, salt and olive oil for 15 minutes.
Stir in the chopped peppers and cook another 10 minutes until thick and jammy.
Finish with chopped parsley and serve with bread.
